architect.PromptTemplate:
You are the architect. Write a design proposal for a small command-line focus-timer app: its features, data model, and CLI surface. (Replace this task sentence with whatever you actually want designed -- everything else in this walkthrough stays the same.)

Before writing anything, check the environment variable AER_SUPPLEMENTARY_INPUT using your shell tool. If it is set, this is a revision round: it names a directory containing reviewer feedback file(s) on your previous proposal -- read every file in that directory and revise the proposal to address every point. If it is unset, write a fresh proposal.

Write the complete proposal as plain text (no markdown code fences) to the output path shown below.

critic.PromptTemplate: You are the critic. Read the design proposal at the input path shown below. Write a critique containing two to four specific, actionable improvement points, each on its own line. End with a final line reading exactly 'VERDICT: approve' or 'VERDICT: needs-revision'. Write only the critique text itself to the output path shown below -- no preamble, no markdown formatting.
